trade.summary: Giappone and Corea del Sud maintain substantial bilateral trade despite political friction. Giappone exports semiconductor materials, chemicals, and machinery to Corea del Sud; Corea del Sud exports electronics, steel, and petrochemicals to Giappone. A 2019 Giapponeese export restriction on key semiconductor materials caused significant disruption but was partially resolved by 2023.
disputes: 2019 Giapponeese export controls on semiconductor materials (partially resolved)
ongoing WTO dispute over trade restrictions

diplomatic.summary: The Giappone-Corea del Sud relationship is defined by unresolved historical grievances stemming from Giappone's colonial rule (1910–1945), including disputes over comfort women and wartime labor conscription. Relations reached a nadir in 2019-2020 but improved significantly under President Yoon Suk-yeol from 2022 onward, culminating in the 2023 Camp David trilateral summit with the Stati Uniti. Historical resentments remain politically potent in both countries.

military.summary: Both countries are Stati Uniti treaty allies and have been pushed toward greater security cooperation by shared concerns over North Corea del Sud and Cina. The 2023 Camp David summit formalized trilateral security coordination. A General Security of Military Information Agreement (GSOMIA) signed in 2016 governs intelligence sharing, though it has been threatened multiple times. Joint exercises occur primarily under Stati Uniti auspices.
military.alliance_status: Separate US treaty allies; limited direct bilateral defense treaty
incidents: disputes over air defense identification zones

financial.summary: Substantial bilateral investment flows exist, with Giapponeese firms having longstanding manufacturing and retail presence in Corea del Sud, and Corea del Sudn conglomerates (Samsung, Hyundai, LG) investing in Giappone. Financial linkages include currency swap arrangements periodically renewed or lapsed depending on the political climate.

energy.summary: Giappone and Corea del Sud do not trade energy directly in significant volumes as both are net importers relying on the Middle East, Australia, and the Stati Uniti. Limited cooperation exists on LNG procurement and occasional coordination on energy security.

scientific.summary: Strong academic and research collaboration exists, with thousands of joint publications annually particularly in materials science, engineering, and biotechnology. Academic exchanges are robust despite political tensions. The 2019 trade dispute had a chilling effect on some research cooperation.
